prompt:
You are an engineering experiment design assistant. Return JSON only.

Task:
{
  "task_id": "task_demo_002",
  "paper_id": "paper_demo_002",
  "domain": "additive_manufacturing",
  "task_type": "experiment_design",
  "goal": "Design one feasible experiment to study how laser power affects porosity in a DED NiTi process.",
  "available_resources": {
    "equipment": [
      "DED machine",
      "SEM",
      "precision saw",
      "mounting press",
      "grinding and polishing kit"
    ],
    "materials": [
      "NiTi powder",
      "argon gas",
      "mounting resin"
    ],
    "constraints": [
      "Use only listed equipment and materials.",
      "Use explicit numeric laser power levels.",
      "Keep all non-target process settings fixed."
    ]
  },
  "evidence_chunk_ids": [
    "paper_002_chunk_01"
  ],
  "evidence_summary": "The DED NiTi process uses argon shielding and a validated stable operating window for laser power. Samples can be sectioned, mounted, ground, and polished for SEM cross-section imaging to quantify porosity.",
  "evidence": [
    {
      "source_id": "paper_002_chunk_01",
      "text": "The DED NiTi process uses argon shielding and a validated stable operating window for laser power. Samples can be sectioned, mounted, ground, and polished for SEM cross-section imaging to quantify porosity."
    }
  ]
}

    {
      "source_id": "paper_005_chunk_01",
      "text": "Hatch spacing can be varied within a stable DED NiTi window, and polished cross-sections can be used for microhardness mapping after preparation with the listed metallography tools."

}
